title: improve more: Canada currently lacks formal and large-scale mechanisms for the reimbursement of Digital Health Technolog…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: improve more: Canada currently lacks formal and large-scale mechanisms for the reimbursement of Digital Health Technologies and Artificial Intelligence. However, recent developments such as the COVID-19 pandemic have led to increased coverage of digitally provided services, including telehealth. Despite these developments, reimbursement for digital therapeutics remains a significant concern in Canada(Vervoort et al., 2022).

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Canada is currently facing a significant gap in terms of formal and large-scale mechanisms for the reimbursement of Digital Health Technologies and Artificial Intelligence. While recent events such as the COVID-19 pandemic have resulted in increased coverage of digitally provided services, including telehealth, there remains a distinct lack of reimbursement frameworks for digital therapeutics. This represents a pressing concern for the Canadian health system, as the potential benefits of digital health technologies and artificial intelligence in the treatment of a wide range of illnesses and conditions is well-documented. In order to ensure that patients have access to the full range of healthcare services they require, it is essential that Canada develops robust reimbursement mechanisms for these technologies as a matter of urgency.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: To date, there is no evidence whether such incentives as social factors actually have had a significant impact on patients’ attitudes towards the use of digital therapeutics. In this regard, various theories and models have been developed to understand and study the affecting factors of technology adoption, such as the Theory of Planned Behavior (TPB), the Theory of Reasoned Action (TRA), and the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM). These theories and models have focused on the individual’s attitude towards certain behaviors, including adopting and utilizing new technologies. Many studies have tried to adapt technology acceptance models in the field of health care, including m-health apps. The adapted models mainly have focused on the acceptance of digital health apps; however, continuous acceptance is still a major issue. Studies have shown that most patients abandon health apps already after relatively few weeks and after only a few uses, while most digital health apps require continued usage over a longer period to positively affect a patient’s health, (Vaghefi and Tulu 2019). Thus, it is largely unknown:

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: To date, it remains unclear whether social factors, such as incentives, have had a significant impact on patients’ attitudes towards the utilization of digital therapeutics. As such, various theories and models have been developed to comprehend and examine the influencing factors of technology adoption, such as the Theory of Planned Behavior (TPB), the Theory of Reasoned Action (TRA), and the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM). These theoretical frameworks aim to highlight an individual's attitude towards particular behaviors, including their willingness to adopt and utilize new technologies. Although many studies have tried to apply technology acceptance models in the healthcare domain, particularly in the context of m-health apps, continuous utilization remains a significant challenge. Research has shown that most patients tend to abandon health apps within a few weeks and after using them only a few times. This is problematic since most digital health apps require ongoing usage over an extended period to positively impact a patient’s health (Vaghefi and Tulu 2019). Hence, it remains unclear:

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: The impact of social factors on patients' attitudes towards the use of digital therapeutics is unclear, and various theoretical frameworks like TPB, TRA, and TAM have been developed to study the influencing factors of technology adoption. However, most patients tend to abandon health apps after only a few weeks of usage, resulting in continuous utilization being a significant challenge for digital health apps.
